identity.name: Golem Network
identity.one_liner: Peer-to-peer marketplace for computing power, connecting providers with requestors via the Yagna framework on Ethereum and Polygon.

founding_team.name: Julian Zawistowski
founding_team.background: Original CEO. Departed to establish Golem Foundation with $40M+ in ETH/GNT.

founding_team.name: Piotr Janiuk
founding_team.background: Mathematics/CS, University of Warsaw. Leading Golem Factory since 2019.

founding_team.name: Aleksandra Skrzypczak
founding_team.background: Now managing partner at Golem Factory.

founding_team.name: Andrzej Regulski
founding_team.background: Original COO. Departed with Zawistowski to Golem Foundation.

rounds.notes: 820,000 ETH raised in under an hour. Third-largest platform ICO at the time. 82% of tokens to ICO participants.

team.leadership_split: In 2019, co-founders Zawistowski and Regulski departed to establish the Golem Foundation, a separate non-profit. Over $40M in ETH and GNT was transferred from Factory to Foundation.

technical.architecture_summary: Peer-to-peer compute marketplace. Yagna framework (Rust) handles task negotiation, execution, and payment. Providers share CPU/GPU resources, Requestors consume compute. Payments on Ethereum mainnet and Polygon L2. Deposit mechanism (v0.16) enables smart contract pre-payment locks.
technical.consensus_mechanism: N/A — uses Ethereum and Polygon for settlement. Peer-to-peer task matching.
technical.what_is_onchain: Token transfers, payment settlement, deposit contracts (Ethereum/Polygon).
technical.what_is_offchain: Task negotiation, compute execution, provider discovery, task distribution.
technical.dev_activity_note: Last yagna commit October 2025 — approximately 5 months of apparent inactivity on core repo. Modelserve AI (key AI product) development temporarily suspended.

token_utility: Payment for compute (sole currency between requestors and providers)
Smart contract deposits for compute pre-payment
tokenomics.protocol_commission: 0% — Golem captures no revenue from network usage
tokenomics.migration_note: Migrated from GNT to GLM (1:1, no deadline). ~796M GLM on Ethereum, remainder still unmigrated GNT.

network_metrics.revenue_note: 0% commission — protocol captures zero revenue from usage.
network_metrics.usage_claim: 428% YoY usage growth and 80%+ utilisation in 2025 — not independently verified.

key_partnerships.detail: Mirroring Salad commercial workloads onto Golem execution layer (Jan 2026)

infrastructure_decentralisation.evidence: Permissionless P2P network — anyone can join as provider or requestor. Providers run own hardware. Payments on Ethereum and Polygon. Relay infrastructure and task matching involve some centralised coordination. Provider count modest vs competitors.

governance_decentralisation.evidence: GAPs governance mechanism since 2020. In practice Golem Factory GmbH retains control over protocol development and treasury. 2019 split created two entities (Factory + Foundation) distributing power somewhat. No formal DAO. Ecosystem Fund allocation team-directed.

token_distribution_fairness.evidence: 82% to ICO participants — one of the fairest distributions in crypto history. Only 18% team/company. Fully circulated, no remaining unlocks. $40M+ transfer to Foundation concentrates resources.

censorship_resistance.evidence: Explicitly designed as censorship-resistant computing. Permissionless, no KYC. Arbitrary workloads supported. Providers choose tasks. Small network scale means coordinated disruption possible.

data_sovereignty.evidence: Requestors send tasks to providers — data leaves requestor's machine. Plans for VPN and SGX but not in production. No TEE/confidential computing currently. Arkiv extends data sovereignty concepts.

open_source_transparency.evidence: All core code GPL-3.0. 100+ repos, 76+ contributors on yagna. OpenZeppelin audited token contract. Team identifiable and public. Dev cadence slowed (5-month gap on yagna). Foundation activities less transparent.

token_utility.evidence: GLM is sole payment currency for compute. Deposit mechanism adds smart contract locking. Arkiv extends to data writes. No staking, no governance weight, no fee-sharing. Pure medium of exchange — substitutable in theory.

value_accrual.evidence: No burn, no staking, no protocol fee capture, no revenue sharing. Token value depends entirely on compute demand. High token velocity — providers sell immediately. Deposit mechanism creates temporary lock-up only.

supply_dynamics.evidence: Fixed 1B supply, no inflation, no future emissions, no remaining unlocks. FDV = market cap. 82% distributed to public in 2016. Clean structure. No deflationary mechanism or staking lock-up.

revenue_sustainability.evidence: 0% commission — protocol captures zero revenue from usage. Pricing at $0.001-$0.01/core-hour is extremely low. Sustainability depends on treasury/Foundation assets. Salad partnership is engineering test only. No transparent runway disclosure.

liquidity_access.evidence: 44 exchanges including Binance, Coinbase, OKX, KuCoin, Upbit. $5-9M daily volume. Good coverage for $133M market cap. Available on Uniswap for decentralised access.
